meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kits to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how a SaaS starter kit, combined with Antigravity and Claude Code, helps students build and deploy professional-grade apps.
content.answer_block: Debugging hell is the primary barrier to shipping your first SaaS. By utilizing a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you inherit battle-tested architecture, eliminating common configuration errors. Combined with AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a frustrated student into a high-velocity, professional-grade developer.
content.intro:
The screen is glowing, your terminal is a graveyard of stack traces, and the deadline for your capstone project is looming. You’ve spent six hours chasing a ghost in your authentication middleware, feeling the familiar sting of imposter syndrome creeping in. You aren't failing; you're just fighting a war with outdated manual setups.

Professional developers don't write boilerplate—they leverage high-leverage frameworks that handle the heavy lifting. By switching to a robust SaaS starter kit, you effectively outsource the infrastructure complexity, allowing you to focus purely on product logic. It’s time to move past the syntax errors and start building the features that will land you your first job in tech.

steps.title: Select a Production-Grade Starter Kit
steps.description: Choose a stack (e.g., Next.js, Supabase, Stripe) that provides pre-built auth and database schemas so you don't build from zero.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and identify which modules are causing the most frequent runtime crashes.

steps.title: Automate Environment Configuration
steps.description: Use your starter kit's CLI to sync environment variables, preventing the 'works on my machine' syndrome.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure .env.example file based on my current database and API integration requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'validate environment variables against requirements'

steps.title: Implement AI-Assisted Error Logging
steps.description: Integrate logging hooks into your starter kit to catch errors before they reach the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function for my starter kit that captures stack traces and explains them in plain English.

steps.title: Refactor Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform deep-dives into complex functions that keep failing under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this asynchronous function to handle edge cases and prevent memory leaks.

steps.title: Deploy to Staging for Automated Testing
steps.description: Push your code to a preview environment to ensure your fixes hold up in a real-world scenario.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my deployment pipeline configuration for potential security vulnerabilities.

pro_tips: Treat your starter kit as a living dependency; pull updates weekly to keep your security patches current.
Always force AI tools to explain 'why' a fix works, not just 'what' the fix is, to accelerate your learning curve.
Use a local database container (like Docker) to mimic production environments precisely, avoiding cloud-latency debugging.
If you find yourself debugging the same module twice, abstract it into a reusable utility file immediately.
Prioritize type-safety (TypeScript) in your starter kit; it catches 70% of bugs before the code even executes.
content.next_steps: Once your app is stable, focus on adding one unique feature that showcases your specific problem-solving style to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework and the tools to ship faster than your peers. Stop debugging in the dark and start building the future of the web. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
